recommended_endpoints.use_case: Aggregate dependency health (db, cache, queue, external APIs) for dashboards and triage
K8s/load-balancer liveness probe; never touches deps, 200 while process is up
K8s readiness probe; 503 when any required dependency is unreachable
Slow-start probe (migrations, warmup); succeeds only after first-time init completes
Canary deployment gating; compare against stable cohort pre-rollout
Build provenance for SRE incident response and release tagging
User-facing status summary linking to incident tracker (Statuspage-style)
Cross-service dep graph snapshot for blast-radius and runbook lookup
Prometheus/Mimir scrape endpoint emitting RED metrics

rationale: Tracera's 9-route health surface (live, ready, health, canary, metrics, versions, status, dependencies, search/health) splits process liveness from dep readiness from deployment-gated canary health - the right shape for a mixed K8s/native fleet. /api/v1 prefix plus three auth tiers (none/bearer-mesh/bearer-scraper) lets one OpenAPI fragment and one Prometheus scrape config cover the whole fleet. PhenoDevOps' phenotype-router-monitor partially implements this already (uses /api/v1/health, /api/v1/routers/{id}/metrics); promote to canonical and backport the gap. Components is a name-keyed map so adopters declare their own deps with no schema churn. Canary and dependencies are bearer-protected to keep internal topology out of public responses.
